<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data (single bit).</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        The module utilizes a 4-bit counter (cnt) to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received. Every eight din_serial input, dout_parallel will output. When all 8 serial data bits have been received, the valid output signal is set to 1, indicating that the parallel output data is valid. Otherwise, the valid output signal is set to 0, indicating that the parallel output data is not valid.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</implementation>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="IDLE" value="2'b00"/>
        <state name="DIVIDE" value="2'b01"/>
        <state name="DONE" value="2'b10"/>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="2-bit" initial_value="IDLE">State variable for controlling the division process.</reg>
        <reg id="count" type="3-bit" initial_value="0">Count variable for tracking the number of iterations.</reg>
        <reg id="remainder" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the current remainder during division.</reg>
        <reg id="quotient" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the current quotient during division.</reg>
        <reg id="dividend_reg" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to store the dividend input.</reg>
        <reg id="divisor_reg" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to store the divisor input.</reg>
        <wire id="sub_result" type="9-bit"/> // Result of subtraction
        <wire id="carry_out" type="1-bit"/> // Carry out from subtraction
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                state <= IDLE;
                count <= 0;
                remainder <= 0;
                quotient <= 0;
                dividend_reg <= 0;
                divisor_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    IDLE: begin
                        if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                            dividend_reg <= dividend;
                            divisor_reg <= divisor;
                            remainder <= {1'b0, dividend}; // Shift dividend left by 1
                            state <= DIVIDE;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    DIVIDE: begin
                        if (count == 8) begin
                            state <= DONE;
                        end else begin
                            // Perform subtraction
                            sub_result = remainder + {1'b0, ~divisor_reg} + 1;
                            carry_out = sub_result[8]; // Check carry out
                            
                            // Update remainder and quotient
                            remainder <= {sub_result[7:0], carry_out};
                            quotient <= {quotient[6:0], carry_out};
                            count <= count + 1;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    DONE: begin
                        state <= IDLE;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <step>On receiving a positive edge of the clock, check if the reset signal is active. If so, reset all registers and outputs to their initial values.</step>
        <step>When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, save the inputs dividend and divisor into respective registers and initialize the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit. Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor. Set the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.</step>
        <step>During the division process, if start_cnt is high, check if the counter cnt has reached 8 (indicating completion). If so, clear cnt and start_cnt, update the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient, and transition back to IDLE state. Otherwise, increment the counter cnt and update the shift register SR based on the subtraction result, performing the necessary shifts and updates accordingly.</step>
        <step>After completing the division process, manage the validity of the result. When res_valid is high and res_ready is also high, set res_valid to 0. Otherwise, if res_valid is high, set res_valid to 1. This ensures that the result is marked as valid until it is consumed by the system.</step>
    </task>
</module>

module width_8to16(
    input clk, input rst_n, input valid_in, input [7:0] data_in,
    output reg valid_out, output reg [15:0] data_out
); 
    reg flag;
    reg [7:0] data_lock;
    
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(!rst_n) begin
            data_out <= 16'd0;
        end
        else begin
            if(valid_in && flag) begin
                data_out <= {data_lock,data_in};
            end
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(!rst_n) begin
            valid_out <= 1'b0;
        end
        else begin
            if(valid_in && flag) begin
                valid_out <= 1'b1;
            end
            else begin
                valid_out <= 1'b0;
            end
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(!rst_n) begin
            flag <= 1'b0;
        end
        else begin
            if(valid_in && !flag) begin
                flag <= 1'b1;
            end
            else if(valid_in && flag) begin
                flag <= 1'b0;
            end
        end
    end
    
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(!rst_n) begin
            data_lock <= 8'd0;
        end
        else begin
            if(valid_in && !flag) begin
                data_lock <= data_in;
            end
        end
    end
endmodule

samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light system with red, yellow, and green indicators, controlled by a pedestrian button. The system operates in four states: idle, red, yellow, and green, managing the timing of each state based on the clock and pedestrian requests.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for pedestrians to cross.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">An 8-bit output representing the count value of the internal counter.</output>
        <output id="red">Red signal indicating the traffic light status.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow signal indicating the traffic light status.</output>
        <output id="green">Green signal indicating the traffic light status.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="idle">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="s1_red">State 1: Red light.</state>
        <state id="s2_yellow">State 2: Yellow light.</state>
        <state id="s3_green">State 3: Green light.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="cnt" width="8">Internal counter for timing control.</register>
        <register id="state" width="2">Current state of the traffic light controller.</register>
        <register id="p_red" width="1">Previous red signal state.</register>
        <register id="p_yellow" width="1">Previous yellow signal state.</register>
        <register id="p_green" width="1">Previous green signal state.</register>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    idle: begin
                        state <= s1_red;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if (pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if (!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if (!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign clock = cnt;
        
        // Update previous states
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
            end else begin
                p_red <= red;
                p_yellow <= yellow;
                p_green <= green;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

    <description>Unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Chip clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="start">Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="ain" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand (a) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <input id="bin" width_description="[15:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier (b) with a data width of 16 bits.</input>
        <output id="yout" width_description="[31:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits.</output>
        <output id="done">Chip output flag signal. Defined as 1 indicates the completion of the multiplication operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Multiplies two 16-bit unsigned numbers using shift-and-add method. Resets on rst_n low, updates on clk rising edge, and sets done flag after 16 shifts.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="parallel2serial">
    <description>Parallel-to-serial converter, converting 4 parallel bits to 1 serial bit output (MSB first). Output valid_out indicates valid serial output. When valid_out = 1, the next bit is outputted in the next 3 cycles.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal. Active low (0) means reset active, while 1 means reset inactive.</input>
        <input id="d" width_description="[3:0]">4-bit parallel data input.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output.</output>
        <output id="dout">Serial output representing the converted data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) reaches 3, it updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1. Otherwise, it increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit. Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_8bit">
    <description>Implement an 8-bit multiplier using shifting and adding operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[7:0]">First 8-bit input operand (multiplicand).</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[7:0]">Second 8-bit input operand (multiplier).</input>
        <output id="product" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the product of A and B.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Initialize product to zero
        product = 16'b0;
        
        // Loop through each bit of the multiplier
        for (i = 0; i < 8; i = i + 1) begin
            // Check if the current bit of the multiplier is 1
            if (B[i] == 1'b1) begin
                // Add the multiplicand shifted left by the current index
                product = product + (A << i);
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects the sequence '10011' in the input IN. Outputs MATCH=1 when the sequence is detected, and MATCH=0 otherwise. Supports continuous input and looping detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to be detected.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating whether the sequence '10011' has been detected.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always @(posedge CLK or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                MATCH <= 0;
            end else begin
                case ({IN, MATCH})
                    2'b10: MATCH <= 0;
                    2'b11: MATCH <= 1;
                    default: MATCH <= 0;
                endcase
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Frequency Divider for generating 50MHz, 10MHz, and 1MHz clocks from a 100MHz input clock.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal (100MHz).</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal (50MHz).</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal (10MHz).</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal (1MHz).</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Counter for 50MHz
        reg [31:0] cnt_50;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_50 <= 0;
            end else begin
                cnt_50 <= cnt_50 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Generate CLK_50
        reg CLK_50_reg;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                CLK_50_reg <= 0;
            end else if (cnt_50 == 32'd1) begin
                CLK_50_reg <= ~CLK_50_reg;
            end
        end
        assign CLK_50 = CLK_50_reg;
        
        // Counter for 10MHz
        reg [31:0] cnt_10;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_10 <= 0;
            end else begin
                cnt_10 <= cnt_10 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Generate CLK_10
        reg CLK_10_reg;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                CLK_10_reg <= 0;
            end else if (cnt_10 == 32'd9) begin
                CLK_10_reg <= ~CLK_10_reg;
                cnt_10 <= 0;
            end
        end
        assign CLK_10 = CLK_10_reg;
        
        // Counter for 1MHz
        reg [31:0] cnt_1;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_1 <= 0;
            end else begin
                cnt_1 <= cnt_1 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Generate CLK_1
        reg CLK_1_reg;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                CLK_1_reg <= 0;
            end else if (cnt_1 == 32'd99) begin
                CLK_1_reg <= ~CLK_1_reg;
                cnt_1 <= 0;
            end
        end
        assign CLK_1 = CLK_1_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Edge detection module for detecting rising and falling edges of input signal a.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal to detect edges.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                rise <= 0;
                down <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (a && !prev_a) begin
                    rise <= 1;
                    down <= 0;
                end else if (!a && prev_a) begin
                    rise <= 0;
                    down <= 1;
                end else begin
                    rise <= 0;
                    down <= 0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_16bit">
    <description>Implement a 16-bit full adder in combinational logic using 8-bit adders.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit input operand B.</input>
        <input id="Cin">Carry-in input.</input>
        <output id="y" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="Co">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Instantiate two 8-bit adders for the upper and lower halves
        wire [7:0] sum_upper;
        wire carry_out_upper;
        wire [7:0] sum_lower;
        wire carry_out_lower;
        
        // First 8-bit adder
        assign {carry_out_upper, sum_upper} = a[15:8] + b[15:8] + Cin;
        
        // Second 8-bit adder
        assign {carry_out_lower, sum_lower} = a[7:0] + b[7:0] + carry_out_upper;
        
        // Combine results
        assign y[15:8] = sum_upper;
        assign y[7:0] = sum_lower;
        assign Co = carry_out_lower;
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers to store intermediate values and control the multiplication process.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="size" value="4">Data width for inputs and outputs.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        Extension of input signals:
        The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
        
        Multiplication operation:
        The module uses a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.
        For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
        If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
        If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0').
        
        Add of partial products:
        The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.
        On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
        
        Final product calculation:
        On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="8">Data width of the FIFO.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="16">Depth of the FIFO.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instantiation
        <instance id="ram" module="dual_port_RAM" width="WIDTH" depth="DEPTH">
            <port id="wclk" connection="wclk"/>
            <port id="wenc" connection="wen"/>
            <port id="waddr" connection="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="wdata" connection="wdata"/>
            <port id="rclk" connection="rclk"/>
            <port id="renc" connection="ren"/>
            <port id="raddr" connection="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="rdata" connection="rdata"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Write pointer management
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <always_comb>
                if (winc && wrstn) begin
                    waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
                end else if (!wrstn) begin
                    waddr_bin <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <wire id="wptr" width="4">
            {waddr_bin[3], waddr_bin[3]^waddr_bin[2], waddr_bin[2]^waddr_bin[1], waddr_bin[1]^waddr_bin[0]}
        </wire>
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width="4">
            <always_comb>
                if (winc && wrstn) begin
                    wptr_buff <= wptr;
                end else if (!wrstn) begin
                    wptr_buff <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <assign wen = winc && wrstn;>
        <assign waddr = waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0];>
        
        // Read pointer management
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <always_comb>
                if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                    raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
                end else if (!rrstn) begin
                    raddr_bin <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <wire id="rptr" width="4">
            {raddr_bin[3], raddr_bin[3]^raddr_bin[2], raddr_bin[2]^raddr_bin[1], raddr_bin[1]^raddr_bin[0]}
        </wire>
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width="4">
            <always_comb>
                if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                    rptr_buff <= rptr;
                end else if (!rrstn) begin
                    rptr_buff <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <assign ren = rinc && rrstn;>
        <assign raddr = raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0];>
        
        // Full/Empty detection
        <assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[2:0]});>
        <assign rempty = (rptr == wptr);>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Asynchronously manages read and write operations using dual-port RAM and pointer buffers.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_32bit">
    <description>Implement a 32-bit carry-lookahead adder using the Carry-Lookahead Adder (CLA) architecture.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="A" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="B" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="S" width_description="[32:1]">32-bit output representing the sum of A and B.</output>
        <output id="C32">Carry-out output.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Instantiate two 16-bit CLA blocks for the first and second halves of the 32-bit inputs
        wire [15:1] S1;
        wire C16;
        cla_block clablock1 (.A(A[16:1]), .B(B[16:1]), .Cin(1'b0), .Sum(S1), .Cout(C16));
        
        wire [15:1] S2;
        wire C32;
        cla_block clablock2 (.A(A[32:17]), .B(B[32:17]), .Cin(C16), .Sum(S2), .Cout(C32));
        
        // Concatenate the outputs of the two CLA blocks to form the final sum
        assign S = {S2, S1};
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit ripple carry adder with pipelining stages and registers for synchronization.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation.</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal indicating the availability of the result.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Pipeline stages
        reg [63:0] adda_reg;
        reg [63:0] addb_reg;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage1;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage2;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage3;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage4;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage5;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage6;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage7;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage8;
        
        // Registers for enabling pipeline stages
        reg en_stage1;
        reg en_stage2;
        reg en_stage3;
        reg en_stage4;
        reg en_stage5;
        reg en_stage6;
        reg en_stage7;
        reg en_stage8;
        
        // Register for output enable signal
        reg o_en_reg;
        
        // Synchronize input enable signal with clock and reset
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                en_stage1 <= 0;
                en_stage2 <= 0;
                en_stage3 <= 0;
                en_stage4 <= 0;
                en_stage5 <= 0;
                en_stage6 <= 0;
                en_stage7 <= 0;
                en_stage8 <= 0;
            end else begin
                en_stage1 <= i_en;
                en_stage2 <= en_stage1;
                en_stage3 <= en_stage2;
                en_stage4 <= en_stage3;
                en_stage5 <= en_stage4;
                en_stage6 <= en_stage5;
                en_stage7 <= en_stage6;
                en_stage8 <= en_stage7;
            end
        end
        
        // Calculate sum values for each pipeline stage
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                adda_reg <= 0;
                addb_reg <= 0;
                sum_stage1 <= 0;
                sum_stage2 <= 0;
                sum_stage3 <= 0;
                sum_stage4 <= 0;
                sum_stage5 <= 0;
                sum_stage6 <= 0;
                sum_stage7 <= 0;
                sum_stage8 <= 0;
            end else begin
                adda_reg <= adda;
                addb_reg <= addb;
                
                if (en_stage1) begin
                    sum_stage1 <= adda_reg + addb_reg;
                end
                
                if (en_stage2) begin
                    sum_stage2 <= sum_stage1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage3) begin
                    sum_stage3 <= sum_stage2;
                end
                
                if (en_stage4) begin
                    sum_stage4 <= sum_stage3;
                end
                
                if (en_stage5) begin
                    sum_stage5 <= sum_stage4;
                end
                
                if (en_stage6) begin
                    sum_stage6 <= sum_stage5;
                end
                
                if (en_stage7) begin
                    sum_stage7 <= sum_stage6;
                end
                
                if (en_stage8) begin
                    sum_stage8 <= sum_stage7;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Update output enable signal based on pipeline stages
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                o_en_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                o_en_reg <= en_stage8;
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign result = {sum_stage8[63], sum_stage8};
        assign o_en = o_en_reg;
    </implementation>
</module>

module pulse_detect(
    input clk, input rst_n, input data_in,
    output reg data_out
);
    reg [2:0] state;

    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(~rst_n) begin
            state <= 3'b000;
            data_out <= 1'b0;
        end
        else begin
            case(state)
                3'b000: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        state <= 3'b001;
                    end
                    else begin
                        state <= 3'b000;
                    end
                end
                3'b001: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        state <= 3'b010;
                    end
                    else begin
                        state <= 3'b001;
                    end
                end
                3'b010: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        state <= 3'b011;
                    end
                    else begin
                        state <= 3'b000;
                    end
                end
                3'b011: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b0) begin
                        state <= 3'b100;
                    end
                    else begin
                        state <= 3'b001;
                    end
                end
                3'b100: begin
                    if(data_in == 1'b1) begin
                        state <= 3'b000;
                    end
                    else begin
                        state <= 3'b000;
                    end
                end
                default: begin
                    state <= 3'b000;
                end
        endcase
    end

    end // Auto-repaired
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if(~rst_n) begin
            data_out <= 1'b0;
        end
        else begin
            if(state == 3'b100 && data_in == 1'b0) begin
                data_out <= 1'b1;
            end
            else begin
                data_out <= 1'b0;
            end
        end
    end

    <description>Serial input data accumulation output, input is 8bit data. The valid_in will be set to 1 before the first data comes in. Whenever the module receives 4 input data, the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results and sets the valid_out to be 1 (will last only 1 cycle).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>

        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>When valid_in is 1, data_in is a valid input. Accumulate four valid input data_in values and calculate the output data_out by adding these four values together. There is no output when there are fewer than four data_in inputs in the interim. Along with the output data_out, a cycle of valid_out=1 will appear as a signal. The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.</implementation>

<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter with specific state transitions.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        On every rising edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the counter value is updated.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the counter value (Q) is set to 0.
        Otherwise, if the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 0, the counter increments by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 1 at the most significant bit position.
        If the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 1, the counter decrements by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 0 at the most significant bit position.
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="RAM">
    <description>Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal to initiate a write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal to initiate a read operation.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the read operation.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output signal representing the data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="6">Width of each memory location in bits.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="8">Number of memory locations in the RAM.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="RAM" width_description="[DEPTH-1:0][WIDTH-1:0]" initial_value="000000">Memory array with 8 locations, each holding 6 bits of data.</reg>
        <reg id="read_data" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]" initial_value="000000">Register to hold the data read from the RAM.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Write operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset all memory locations to 0
                integer i;
                for (i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                    RAM[i] <= 6'b000000;
                end
            end else if (write_en) begin
                // Write data to the specified address
                RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
            end
        end
        
        // Read operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset read_data to 0
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end else if (read_en) begin
                // Read data from the specified address
                read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
            end else begin
                // Clear read_data if read_en is not active
                read_data <= 6'b000000;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer with specific timing requirements.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal of size 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal that controls the selection operation.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal of size 4 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Data Register
        reg [3:0] data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                data_reg <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                data_reg <= data_in;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Data Register
        reg en_data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                en_data_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_data_reg <= data_en;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Control Registers
        reg en_clap_one, en_clap_two;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                en_clap_one <= 1'b0;
                en_clap_two <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
                en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
            end
        end
        
        // Output Assignment
        reg [3:0] dataout;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                dataout <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                if (en_clap_two) begin
                    dataout <= data_reg;
                end else begin
                    dataout <= dataout;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
